_doc: PLACEHOLDER FILE — RENAME to google_oauth_client.json with a real
payload before Gmail ingestion works.
Where to get the real file:
1. console.cloud.google.com → create (or pick) a project
2. APIs & Services → Library → search 'Gmail API' → Enable
3. APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen
- User type: External
- App name, support email, developer contact
- Scopes tab: ADD https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly
- Test users tab: add the Gmail addresses you want to test with
(up to 100 are allowed before verification is required)
4. APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials
- OAuth client ID
- Application type: Web application
- Authorized redirect URIs: http://localhost:8001/api/oauth/google/callback
(ALSO add the production URL if/when you deploy)
5. Click 'Download JSON' → save here as google_oauth_client.json
(drop the `_dummy` suffix). Update PLATFORM_GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_FILE
in .env.local to match.
The shape of the real file matches what's stubbed below — the
Google library auto-loads it via Flow.from_client_secrets_file().
THIS DUMMY FILE WILL NOT AUTHENTICATE. All Gmail routes gracefully
reject with 'gmail not configured' if the client_id starts with
'dummy-'.
